perturbation.original_text: The following capitalized terms will have the meanings set forth after each term: "Affiliate" means any natural person or firm, corporation, partnership, limited liability company, association, trust or other entity which, directly or indirectly, controls, is controlled by, or is under common Control with, the subject entity.
perturbation.changed_text: The following capitalized terms will have the meanings set forth after each term: "Affiliate" means any natural person only.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of "Affiliate" has been restricted to only natural persons. This contradicts the multiple references within the same definition section to "firm, corporation, partnership, limited liability company, association, trust or other entity", where the broader definition is expected to be applied. This in-text contradiction will affect the interpretation of rights and obligations when considering the actions of Affiliates, for example, in Subsection 5.1.15 regarding Competitors, and other sections involving transfers and guarantees.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The following capitalized terms will have the meanings set forth after each term: "Affiliate" means any natural person or firm, corporation, partnership, limited liability company, association, trust or other entity which, directly or indirectly, controls, is controlled by, or is under common Control with, the subject entity. "Agreement" means this Franchise Agreement, including any exhibits, attachments and addenda. "Anti-Corruption Laws" means all applicable anti-corruption, anti-bribery, anti-money laundering, books and records, and internal controls laws of the United States and the United Kingdom, including the United States Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and the United Kingdom Bribery Act of 2010. "Brand" means the brand name set forth in the Addendum.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of "Affiliate" to only natural persons, directly contradicting the original, broader definition in the contradicted text, which includes "firm, corporation, partnership, limited liability company, association, trust or other entity." This contradiction creates uncertainty regarding the interpretation and enforcement of provisions related to Affiliates within the contract, as the scope of entities considered affiliates is no longer consistently defined.

perturbation.original_text: "Gross Rooms Revenue" means all revenues derived from the sale or rental of Guest Rooms (both transient and permanent) of the Hotel, including revenue derived from the redemption of points or rewards under the loyalty programs in which the Hotel participates, amounts attributable to breakfast (where the guest room rate includes breakfast), and guaranteed no-show revenue and credit transactions, whether or not collected, at the actual rates charged, less allowances for any Guest Room rebates and overcharges, and will not include taxes collected directly from patrons or guests. Group booking rebates, if any, paid by you or on your behalf to third-party groups for group stays must be included in, and not deducted from, the calculation of Gross Rooms Revenue.
perturbation.changed_text: "Gross Rooms Revenue" means all revenues derived from the sale or rental of Guest Rooms (both transient and permanent) of the Hotel, including revenue derived from the redemption of points or rewards under the loyalty programs in which the Hotel participates, amounts attributable to breakfast (where the guest room rate includes breakfast), and guaranteed no-show revenue and credit transactions, whether or not collected, at the actual rates charged, including allowances for any Guest Room rebates and overcharges, and will not include taxes collected directly from patrons or guests. Group booking rebates, if any, paid by you or on your behalf to third-party groups for group stays must be included in, and not deducted from, the calculation of Gross Rooms Revenue.
perturbation.explanation: Changed "less allowances for any Guest Room rebates and overcharges" to "including allowances for any Guest Room rebates and overcharges". This creates a contradiction, as previously rebates and overcharges were a reduction, while now, due to the change, they would be counted within the calculation. This directly impacts Section 8.2.4 and 10.2 related to Monthly Fees and reports that use Gross Room Revenue.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event of fire or other insured casualty that results in a reduction of Gross Rooms Revenue, you will determine and pay us, from the proceeds of any business interruption or other insurance applicable to loss of revenues, an amount equal to the forecasted Monthly Fees, based on the Gross Rooms Revenue amounts agreed on between you and your insurance company that would have been paid to us in the absence of such casualty.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines Gross Rooms Revenue by subtracting allowances for rebates and overcharges. The changed text includes these allowances, directly increasing the Gross Rooms Revenue. Section 8.2.4 states that Monthly Fees are based on Gross Rooms Revenue. Because the Gross Rooms Revenue is now calculated differently, it changes the value of the Monthly Fees, creating a direct conflict. The contradicted text relies on a definition of Gross Rooms Revenue which has been altered to include amounts that were previously excluded, thereby creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 14.2 Immediate Termination by Us. We may immediately terminate this Agreement on notice to you and without any opportunity to cure the default if: 14.2.1 after curing any material breach of this Agreement or the Standards, you engage in the same non-compliance within any consecutive twenty-four (24) month period, whether or not the non-compliance is corrected after notice, which pattern of non-compliance in and of itself will be deemed material; 14.2.2 you receive three (3) notices of material default in any twelve (12) month period, even if the defaults have been cured;
perturbation.changed_text: 14.2 Immediate Termination by Us. We may immediately terminate this Agreement on notice to you and without any opportunity to cure the default if: 14.2.1 after curing any material breach of this Agreement or the Standards, you engage in the same non-compliance within any consecutive twenty-four (24) month period, whether or not the non-compliance is corrected after notice, which pattern of non-compliance in and of itself will be deemed immaterial; 14.2.2 you receive three (3) notices of material default in any twelve (12) month period, even if the defaults have been cured;
perturbation.explanation: Changed 'deemed material' to 'deemed immaterial' in the context of repeated non-compliance. This creates an in-text contradiction. The initial phrase suggests that repeated non-compliance is a significant issue and grounds for immediate termination, but now the text specifies that repeated non-compliance is deemed immaterial. This directly contradicts the intended meaning of this clause to quickly resolve conflicts. This also affects other sections of the document that directly rely on section 14.2.
perturbation.contradicted_text: We may immediately terminate this Agreement on notice to you and without any opportunity to cure the default if: 14.2.1 after curing any material breach of this Agreement or the Standards, you engage in the same non-compliance within any consecutive twenty-four (24) month period, whether or not the non-compliance is corrected after notice, which pattern of non-compliance in and of itself will be deemed immaterial; 14.2.2 you receive three (3) notices of material default in any twelve (12) month period, even if the defaults have been cured; 14.2.3 you fail to pay debts as they become due or admit in writing your inability to pay your debts or you make a general assignment for the benefit of your creditors;
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters 'deemed material' to 'deemed immaterial' regarding repeated non-compliance after a cure. This directly contradicts the idea that repeated non-compliance warrants immediate termination. This creates a direct conflict with the overall purpose of the clause and causes an in-text contradiction in that the same action is both grounds for termination and not material.
